markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Black Powder Knuckle Dusters](/Weapons.aspx?ID=214)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 159

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This pair of [knuckle dusters](/Weapons.aspx?ID=185) is fitted with an explosive charge of black powder within the hollowed spikes of the weapon and a firing mechanism you hold in your hand while in use.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d4 B

**Group** [Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d8" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=656" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d4 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />
name: Black Powder Knuckle Dusters (Melee)

>
[Dagger Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=216)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This weapon, favored by [rangers](/Classes.aspx?ID=9) and other wilderness wanderers, takes the form of a stoutly built pistol with a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) blade attached beneath the barrel and extending out past the muzzle.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Thrown 10 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=195" />
<trait label="Versatile S" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=200" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d4 P

**Group** [Knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gnome Amalgam Musket](/Weapons.aspx?ID=217)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 159

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

Rumored to be the result of a [gnomish](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3) dare to make a variant of a [hooked hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=52) that's even more intricate and complex, this weapon adds a musket to the mix of an already overcomplicated device.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Backswing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=545" />
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />
<trait label="Gnome" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=80" />
<trait label="Trip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=196" />
<trait label="Versatile P" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=200" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d8 B

**Group** [Hammer](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d10" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
<trait label="Gnome" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=80" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 50 ft.

**Reload** 2
</row>

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This weapon, favored by [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) and those who like smashing and shooting, takes the form of a stoutly built gun designed similarly to an [arquebus](/Weapons.aspx?ID=195) with a hammer head built into the muzzle, decreasing kickback but limiting range.

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mace Multipistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=220)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 159

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 13 gp

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Hands** 1

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

At first glance this weapon looks like nothing more than an iron-bound [club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=2). But the top of the weapon features a latch that opens to reveal three rotating pistol muzzles concealed in the mace's head that can be fired and rotated using triggers built into the weapon's haft.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=602" />
<trait label="Shove" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=193" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 B

**Group** [Club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Capacity 3" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=399" />
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d8" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d4 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 20 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

Favored by caravan guards who traverse the Mana Wastes, a piercing wind is similar to a [jezail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=203), in that you can carry it in one hand as long as the other hand's free, by holding it under one arm. Additionally, it's fitted with an underslung curved blade.

---

An explosive dogslicer is a sneaky, explosive weapon that often brings perverse joy to the [goblins](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=4) who use them. At first glance, it appears to be a triple-bladed [dogslicer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=49) with an oversized guard.

>
[Spoon Gun](/Weapons.aspx?ID=225)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cobbled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=400" />
<trait label="Goblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=81" />
<trait label="Modular B, P, or S" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=271" />
<trait label="Scatter 5 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=413" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 157

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d6 modular

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

**Range** 30 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

</column>

---

No one's entirely certain who developed the spoon gun, but all authorities agree that it was probably a goblin. Essentially a terrible idea in firearm form, the spoon gun is a spring-powered [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193) with a modified grip that uses miscellaneous knives, forks, chopsticks, and spoons as ammunition. Users typically upend the entire contents of their cutlery drawer into the gun, aim it in the general direction of the foe, and hope it hits something.

 This hand cannon is a martial weapon, instead of a simple weapon. It has the [scatter](/Traits.aspx?ID=413) (5 feet) trait and uses cutlery or similar-sized objects as ammunition instead of bullets (enough cheap cutlery to fire ten shots costs 1 sp). 

Big boom guns and spoon guns are modified versions  of hand cannons with potential benefits and drawbacks  alike, rather than separate weapons. If you have access  to the spoon gun or big boom gun modifications, you  can purchase them whenever you would purchase a hand cannon, and effects that depend upon and apply to hand  cannons apply to both weapons.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cobbled" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=400" />
<trait label="Fatal d12" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=98) pg. 157

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon weapons, ammunition, and related items from Chapter 4_

**Access**  The following regions have access to firearms: Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, Tian Xia, Vudra, Arcadia, Ustalav, the Shackles

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 10 gp

**Damage** 1d6 modular

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Range** 20 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Firearm Ammunition (10 rounds)](/Weapons.aspx?ID=211)

</column>

---

Developed by a goblin weaponsmith who missed the ‘hand' part of ‘hand cannon', this comically-oversized gun has a heavily reinforced barrel and is loaded with a worrisome quantity of gunpowder. This [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193) is a martial weapon, instead of a simple weapon. It has the fatal d12 trait and a range increment of 20 feet. It also has the following modified critical failure condition:

 **Critical Failure** The attack misses, the weapon misfires, and you take 1d12 fire damage as it explodes in your face. 

Big boom guns and spoon guns are modified versions  of hand cannons with potential benefits and drawbacks  alike, rather than separate weapons. If you have access  to the spoon gun or big boom gun modifications, you  can purchase them whenever you would purchase a hand cannon, and effects that depend upon and apply to hand  cannons apply to both weapons.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

>
[Clockwork Macuahuitl](/Weapons.aspx?ID=227)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Backswing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=173" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Forceful" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=180" />
<trait label="Versatile B" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=200" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 22

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 550 gp

**Damage** 1d10 S

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5)
</row>

</column>

---

This finely-made wooden club has a beautiful, lacquered finish that gleams in the sunlight. A heavy ring of gears lined with sharpened pieces of obsidian automatically and constantly spin around the bulk of the wooden club lengthwise. Striking a foe digs the obsidian gears into the enemy's flesh and tears it with its blades. The ever-turning gears also help to dislodge an enemy's defensive position against the weapon.

 The clockwork macuahuitl deals 1d10 slashing damage and has the backswing, forceful, and versatile B traits. The clockwork macuahuitl is a two-handed advanced weapon in the club weapon group.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

A griffon cane is named for the shape of its base, which features four small pronged supports splayed out in a manner similar to a griffon's talons. A griffon cane's splayed foot enables it to stand upright by itself. A griffon cane deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage, has the [backswing](/Traits.aspx?ID=173) and [two-hand d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=198) traits, and is a martial melee weapon in the [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5) weapon group.

</row>

</column>

---

Your cane indicates that you have low vision or are blind. By holding this cane in front of you, you indicate to those around that you're partially sighted, which is particularly useful in urban or busy places to let others know to give you enough space. The cane is typically several feet long, generally reaching the user's chin, but can be lengthened or shortened as needed.

 Probing canes are made from reinforced wood with the same strengthening process and treatment as a longbow, creating a cane that is both high quality and durable enough for an adventuring owner to use as a weapon. A probing cane deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage, has the finesse and sweep traits, and is a martial melee weapon in the club weapon group.

A set of large blades have been attached to your chair's wheels. While in the chair, you gain a wheel blade Strike. Your wheel blade deals 1d4 slashing damage and has the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [attached](/Traits.aspx?ID=171), and [free-hand](/Traits.aspx?ID=181) traits. A wheel blade is a simple melee weapon in the [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15) weapon group. You can etch weapon runes onto wheel blades. A wheelchair can only have one attached weapon.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

The asp coil, named both for its slithering striking style and its usage among Aspis Consortium agents, has two forms. In sword form, it resembles an elegant, oddly balanced sword. However, with a twist of the pommel, the blade splits into a series of segments connected by elaborate metal cables.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Orc" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=124" />
<trait label="Shove" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=193" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 105

</row>

</column>

---

Invented by Belkzen's zealous Steel-Eaters, the butchering axe has an oversized head and a long, thick haft counterbalanced with steel or stone. The weapon's sweeping strokes inflict immense damage, particularly against groups of foes, and can push dangerous opponents away to a safe distance. All of these qualities are particularly useful against the lumbering zombie hordes of the Whispering Tyrant. Correspondingly, butchering axes are often wielded by orc and half-orc Crimson Reclaimers of Lastwall.

You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

This spear has a spade-like blade at one end and a forked blade at the other, making it resemble a large arrow. It's well balanced for spinning and twisting maneuvers. >
[Hongali Hornbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=238)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Deadly d6" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=174" />
<trait label="Propulsive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=191" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128, [Grand Bazaar](/Sources.aspx?ID=100) pg. 105

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Tian Xia have access to this option_

**Access** Hongali characters have access to the Hongali hornbow.

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 11 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1+

**Range** 40 ft.

**Reload** 0
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)
</row>

</column>

---

These immense bows are traditionally made from the horns of great beasts, though modern residents of Hongal, the northernmost nation in Tian Xia, often use composite materials or even small trees as the weapon's base. While Hongali hornbows have a shorter range than other bows, they make up for it by focusing the power of the longbow at a short distance and can be used from horseback—perfect for the skirmishing fighting style of mounted Hongali troops. While it's difficult for those in Avistan to get their hands on these Hongali weapons, a small warband of beast-riding orcs from the Hold of Belkzen managed to arm themselves with Hongali hornbows and cut a bloody swathe through adventurers and neighboring militaries alike for several years before they were finally defeated via a desperate ambush. The stories of that warband spread, causing Avistani adventurers with little knowledge of Tian Xia to associate the bows with orcs.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />

</column>

---

A classical weapon of Irrisenis who lacked magical talent, the sickle-saber has seen a resurgence since the coronation of Queen Anastasia. The queen was fascinated with the sickle-sabers in the palace treasury, and now the unusual blades are carried by her honor guard and Irriseni diplomats alike. The sickle-saber's blade curves multiple times along its 4-foot length, and its hilt is similarly curved. A small, secondary grip on the blade lets the wielder rapidly and unpredictably twist the cutting edges.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-15" />

Another complex gnome invention, the switchscythe is designed for versatility. The curved blade is partly hollow, containing a long rod of wood or metal; the rod can be pulled perpendicular to the blade, turning the switchscythe from a sweeping, axe-like blade into a hooked pick capable of grappling a foe.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 1"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Shobhad Longrifle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=251)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Backstabber" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=172" />
<trait label="Concussive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=401" />
<trait label="Fatal d12" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=178" />
<trait label="Kickback" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=409" />
<trait label="Volley 30 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=201" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 20 gp

**Damage** 1d8 P

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 2

**Range** 120 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Firearm](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=16)
</row>

</column>

---

The shobhad longrifle is a firearm with a long, rifled barrel. Often mounted with a multi-lensed scope for targeting at an array of ranges and a chambering mechanism that can adjust the speed and penetrating power of each shot, it's the preferred weapon of many [shobhad](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=296) warriors. Shobhad longrifles are equipped with a built-in [silencer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1206) so they make no more noise than a crossbow when fired. A shobhad longrifle is a martial weapon.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-16" />
name: Shobhad Longrifle

    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bladed Gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=253)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Agile" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=170" />
<trait label="Finesse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=179" />
<trait label="Free-Hand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=181" />
<trait label="Modular B, P, or S" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=271" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Knights of Lastwall](/Sources.aspx?ID=123) pg. 87

**Favored Weapon** [Genzaeri](/Deities.aspx?ID=342)

**Access** Knights of Lastwall have access to this weapon.

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 5 gp

**Damage** 1d4 modular

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4)
</row>

</column>

---

A [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) attached to a retractable mechanism is integrated in this gauntlet's dorsum, so a combatant can quickly arm themself with a blade to exploit the weak points in an enemy's armor. Switching configurations on the gauntlet reveals or retracts the contained dagger as appropriate. The dagger isn't removable, and thus can't be wielded or etched with runes separately from the gauntlet.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-4" />
name: Bladed Gauntlet

The battle saddle is a special saddle for a mount that has two large, winglike blades. These blades normally lie flat alongside the saddle, providing additional protection for the rider, but they can be deployed with a tug on the reins to slash at enemies adjacent to the mount. When using a battle saddle to parry, you can decide whether the circumstance bonus to AC applies to you or to your mount.

Traditionally a favored weapon in Molthune for settling disputes between military leaders, the dancer's spear has seen a recent resurgence in popularity in the neighboring kingdom of Nirmathas, largely due to its effectiveness at striking down attacking [skeletons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=92) and other [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) from a relatively safe distance. A dancer's spear has a 7-foot-long wooden haft capped with a triangular metal blade at one end, counterbalanced on the other end with a reinforced metal sleeve that, in a pinch, can be used as an effective striking surface.

**Favored Weapon** [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=335), [Wheels of Innovation](/Deities.aspx?ID=385)

The polytool is a small metal rod with a number of simple tools folded inside. The user can extend a long ceramic blade, as well as an awl, chisel, file, [flint and steel](/Equipment.aspx?ID=21), hook, inkpen, magnifying glass, pliers, scissors, and a small saw. The flint and steel can be used up to 10 times before needing to be replaced. Though inspired by advanced Numerian technology, the polytool is a simple enough feat of metalworking that any blacksmith could produce it—perfect for the goddess [Casandalee](/Deities.aspx?ID=34) to spread innovation farther than actual Numerian tech could reach.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-15" />

Similar to a [chakram](/Weapons.aspx?ID=235), chakri are too light to be wielded in melee but allow the user significantly more control over their throws. A chakri is small and light enough that up to two can be worn on each wrist; a chakri worn on the wrist is reload 0 instead of reload —.

    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spraysling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=340)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Halfling" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=87" />
<trait label="Propulsive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=191" />
<trait label="Scatter 5 ft." url="/Traits.aspx?ID=413" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 1 gp

**Damage** 1d6 B

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

**Range** 20 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Ranged

**Category** Martial

**Group** [Sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

**Ammunition** [Spray Pellet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=353)

</column>

---

A spraysling is similar to a standard [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=72) but with a wider cup fitted with a thin blade affixed to the cup's edges. When used to make an attack with a specially prepared packet of spray pellets, the razor slices open the packet and the weapon launches a cluster of stinging pellets.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-13" />
name: Spraysling

    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Lancer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=346)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Martial

</column>

---

This lance has a heavy grip with two parallel crossbow fixtures built into it, making it a useful weapon for combats who prefer to keep their distance at all times.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Critical Fusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=402" />
<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=192" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 P

**Group** [Spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Capacity 2" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=399" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d8 P

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 ft.

**Reload** 2
</row>

**Group** [Bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3)

**Ammunition** [Bolts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=81)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-3" />
name: Lancer (Ranged)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Weapon 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wrecker](/Weapons.aspx?ID=348)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Combination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=417" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault (Remastered)](/Sources.aspx?ID=191) pg. 31

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 8 gp

**Bulk** 2
</row>

**Hands** 2

**Category** Advanced

</column>

---

The wrecker combines a [dwarven dorn-dergar](/Weapons.aspx?ID=280) with a heavy, gear-reinforced arm cover that allows it to be fired like an oversized sling, then retrieved and reloaded by manually activating a clockwork spool. A wrecker must be loaded to be switched from its ranged configuration to its melee configuration.

<title level="2">Melee</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Razing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=488" />
<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=192" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d8 B

**Group** [Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)

</column>

<title level="2">Ranged</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />
<trait label="Ranged Trip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=251" />
<trait label="Razing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=488" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Damage** 1d6 B

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 20 ft.

**Reload** 1
</row>

**Group** [Sling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-13" />
name: Wrecker (Ranged)

These unique firearms are designed to attach to shields while still firing normally. Shield pistols are popular among Firebrands in the Shackles as they allow them to hold a blade and a firearm in each hand without giving up on defense. As is normal with firearms, a character doesn't have access to shield pistols unless they separately have access to firearms.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

This very long spear, sometimes called a pike, is purely for thrusting rather than throwing. Used by many soldiers and city watch for crowd control and defense against charging enemies, it must be wielded with two hands.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-14" />

<title level="2">Specific Magic Weapons</title>
[Dragon's Tongue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4012), [Hydra Spear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4004), [Icicle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2868)
name: Longspear

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Dwarf" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=54" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 279

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 3 gp

**Damage** 1d8 S

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1)
</row>

</column>

---

This favored weapon of the [dwarves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1) has a large, ornate head mounted on a thick handle. This powerful axe can be wielded with one hand or two.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).

<document level="2" id="weapon-group-1" />

>
[Gnome Flickmace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=421)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Gnome" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=80" />
<trait label="Reach" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=192" />
<trait label="Sweep" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=708" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=216) pg. 279

**Favored Weapon** [The Pandemonia](/Deities.aspx?ID=370)

<row gap="medium">
**Price** 3 gp

**Damage** 1d6 B

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Hands** 1

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Type** Melee

**Category** Advanced

**Group** [Flail](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=7)
</row>

</column>

---

More a flail than a mace, this weapon has a short handle attached to a length of chain with a ball at the end. The ball is propelled to its reach with the flick of the wrist, the momentum of which brings the ball back to the wielder after the strike.

<title level="2">Critical Specialization Effects</title>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 283

Certain feats, class features, weapon runes, and other effects can grant you additional benefits when you make a Strike with certain weapons and get a critical success. This is called a critical specialization effect. The exact effect depends on which weapon group your weapon belongs to, as listed below. You can always decide not to add the critical specialization effect of your weapon.

[Click here for the full rules on Critical Specialization Effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=235).
